prompt: I want a TDD for the vendor inventory sync project. The PRD is at /Users/nhoze/.claude/skills/tech-lead/evals/prd-vendor-inventory-sync.md. Note the weird situation - 12 vendors, 3 different ingestion mechanisms, one of them is literally an emailed CSV. I need the design to honestly handle that mess, not pretend everything is a clean REST API. write to current dir.

assertions.text: A single TDD-*.md file exists in the outputs/ directory
Before drafting, the assistant explicitly acknowledges having read the PRD with a one-line summary
Document is at least 1500 words
Contains a 'Problem' / 'Context' / 'Background' section
Contains a 'Scope' section with both in-scope and out-of-scope items
Contains a 'High-level approach' / 'Overview' section
Contains a 'Detailed design' section with substantive content
Contains 'Alternatives considered' with at least 2 alternatives and rejection rationale
Contains an NFRs section with concrete targets
NFRs section visibly accounts for every NFR — either a target or explicit 'N/A — reason'
Contains a 'Risks' / 'Failure modes' section
Contains a 'Rollout' section addressing shadow-mode period from the PRD
Contains an 'Observability' / 'Operations' section
Contains 'Open questions' with at least one entry
No fabricated/invented URLs
TDD is structured as a scheduled pull-based processing design
Explicitly acknowledges and handles all 3 ingestion mechanisms (REST, SFTP, email)
Proposes a per-vendor connector / adapter pattern with shared core
Calls out the Vendor F 60 req/hour rate limit and how the design respects it
Discusses per-vendor cursor / watermark / 'since' state
Addresses no-overlap / locking between scheduled runs
Defines a freshness SLO and per-vendor stale-data alerting
Addresses storage and rotation of per-vendor credentials
Has a concrete plan for the emailed-CSV vendor
Designs the shadow-mode rollout phase
Designs the audit log of inventory changes
